rationale: The Pareto front shows best Brier scores cluster around C=0.02-0.09 (higher end helps calibration), row_interval=15-25, and low edge_threshold drives higher equity. The worst trials all have very low C (<0.002), suggesting heavy regularization hurts calibration. I'll tighten C upward, keep row_interval choices, lower edge_threshold range to capture the high-equity region, and slightly raise kelly_mult/max_frac since the best equity trials use higher values. I'll also drop depth_skew/ob_imbalance aliases and add vwap_deviation and rsi_14 to potentially improve signal diversity without collinearity.
